text.title: Business Analysis Planning: Git-Native Workflow and Agent Patterns
text.summary: Git-native BA plan management, replan triggers, recommended subagents, two-shot prompt pattern, and AI gotchas.

section.title: Git-Native Plan Management

rule.statement: Store the BA Approach Document as Markdown with YAML frontmatter (engagement_id, approach, version, approver, last_reviewed) committed to git; diffs become the change log, git blame is the audit trail.
rule.rationale: A BA plan stored only in a wiki or project management tool cannot be versioned, diffed, or audited; git provides all three for free while keeping the plan colocated with the requirements it governs.

rule.statement: Enforce a 14-day cadence via cron + ba-plan-check.py; staleness older than 14 days is the leading indicator of plan-reality drift.
rule.rationale: Plans updated only at the start of engagement and then ignored are the dominant failure mode in BA engagements; automated staleness detection triggers replanning before drift becomes a project risk.

section.reference: Validates BA Approach Document frontmatter and review cadence

rule.statement: Bake replan triggers into the plan document itself (not agent memory) so they fire deterministically: stakeholder added/removed, scope change merged, two missed elicitation sessions, or last_reviewed older than 14 days.
rule.rationale: Replanning triggered by human observation misses events that happen between reviews; deterministic triggers wired to git events, stakeholder register diffs, and calendar checks fire without human attention.

rule.statement: Cap any agent-driven plan update to 5 changes per run and require human review on the diff; a runaway replanner rewriting half the deliverables invalidates in-flight elicitation work.
rule.rationale: Bulk replanning is dangerous because it can silently invalidate stakeholder commitments and elicitation schedules that were built against the previous plan.

example.note: Two-shot BA planning prompt: context read then plan draft. Forces approach decision rules and flags gaps as TODO rather than inventing values.
example.code:
You are the BA planner. Produce a BA Approach Document v0.1 using the template in ba-planning/templates/ba-approach-document.md (sections 1-8). Engagement context: {initiative_brief}. Org culture: {formal|informal|mixed}. Constraints: {time_budget, stakeholder_availability, regulatory}.
Decision rules: - If requirements clarity high AND change frequency low -> Plan-Driven. - If clarity low OR continuous discovery active -> Change-Driven. - Else Hybrid; declare per-artifact which is baselined vs. living.
Return strict JSON matching the template section keys. Do not invent stakeholders not in {stakeholder_seed}. Flag every gap as TODO(reason). No time estimates.

antipattern.description: LLM defaults to Plan-Driven for all engagements regardless of context flags.
antipattern.reason: Models anchor on whichever sample plan they last saw; always pass the engagement model and culture flags in the system prompt and validate that the output's approach matches the decision rule output for the given inputs.

antipattern.description: Agent invents plausible-sounding stakeholders ("Director of UX Excellence") not present in the actual org chart.
antipattern.reason: Hallucinated stakeholders produce engagement plans with no real recipients; constrain to a stakeholder_seed list and flag additions as TODO(reason), not as entries.

antipattern.description: Agent produces time estimates in the plan ("Discovery: 4 weeks").
antipattern.reason: Per repo policy, no time estimates in SDD docs; strip any time estimate from agent output and replace with complexity tier (S/M/L). Time estimates are inherently unreliable and create false expectations.

antipattern.description: Agent conflates BA Planning with Project Planning, producing Gantt charts for BA activities.
antipattern.reason: BA Planning covers analysis activities only; schedule integration and resource management belong to the project-manager skill. Mixing them produces a document that satisfies neither purpose.

rule.statement: Require human approval at four points: plan v1.0 sign-off, every approach change (Plan-Driven / Change-Driven / Hybrid), every change request touching governance, every replan that adds or removes a stakeholder.
rule.rationale: These four points are the decisions that determine what BA work happens and who is responsible for it; agent preparation is valuable, but decision authority must remain with a named human.
